text.tspan: Problem: MCP is about agent-to-TOOL communication.
But what about agent-to-AGENT? Multi-agent systems need agents from
different vendors / frameworks / clouds to discover, negotiate, collaborate.
Agent B (CrewAI, AutoGen, custom, Vertex Agent Builder...)
Agents are peers - either side can request, either side can fulfill.
Different from MCP (which is asymmetric client / server).
AGENT CARD: discovery metadata (capabilities, skills, auth)
TASKS: work units; long-running with status updates
MESSAGES: conversational exchanges within a task
ARTIFACTS: structured outputs the agent produces
Use for: file access, DB queries, API calls
Use for: delegating a sub-task to a specialist agent,
An agent uses MCP for tools AND A2A for sub-agents.
less mature than MCP but growing fast.
